posts.text:
I want to know! Did you use AI during the L.A. wildfires?

mattdpearce.substack.com/p/d…

Columbia Journalism Review (@CJR)

The LA Fires and what a disaster says about our physical and informational environments, by @Jon_Allsop. cjr.org/the_media_today/la_w…




— https://nitter.net/CJR/status/1878829345621659711#m

Matt Pearce 🦅🇺🇸 (@mattdpearce)

TAX ZUCKERBERG, FUND KICK-ASS LOCAL JOURNALISTS 

mattdpearce.substack.com/p/c…



— https://nitter.net/mattdpearce/status/1878607938547585212#m

TAX ZUCKERBERG, FUND KICK-ASS LOCAL JOURNALISTS 

mattdpearce.substack.com/p/c…
Thank you Jonathan!!

Happy to share my latest article for the good folks @newrepublic.com, where I tackle recent shakeups in American journalism and highlight my ongoing teaching on the history of American journalism and the rise of disinformation.

newrepublic.com/article/2075...

For about a year I've been working with some of the best reporters and editors in L.A. to build a brand new city news outlet. It launches tomorrow.

Is it possible to operate a sustainable local journalism business in 2026? Let me know! lamaterial.com

okay, a happy holidays video, we see those all the time

*ten seconds in*

"a british warlord named coroticus"

you have my attention sir

The discourse around OBAA has been funny to me because I went and read “Vineland,” which is much more centrally about the Perfidia character’s tormented romantic relationship with the fascist, TV turning revolutionaries into passive zomboids, and it all ends with Reagan defunding the feds.

“With her nomination for ‘Weapons,’ Madigan set a new record for the longest gap between Oscar nominations for an actress. She earned her last nod 40 years ago for her performance as Sunny in 1985’s ‘Twice in a Lifetime,’ directed by Bud Yorkin and starring Gene Hackman.”

On the rising stress levels out of Hollywood these days: 

mattdpearce.substack.com/p/exit-holly...

"Complex divisions of labor, built upon the febrile intelligences and starving egos of skilled weirdos?...Does David Ellison know he’s counting on artists help pay back the $79 billion in debt for the new Paramount-Warner Brothers goliath?"

mood: "Like some kind of air bubble trapped in an underwater shipwreck, Hollywood is one of those ultraweird American exceptions to the rule, like newsrooms and publishing houses, where the business realities of mass production still cohabitate with the occult predilections of craftspeople."

On the rising stress levels out of Hollywood these days: 

mattdpearce.substack.com/p/exit-holly...

“The humans get up onstage and start thanking everybody, but most typically, in statistical descending order, 1. Their family, 2. God, 3. their agent, and 4. Steven Spielberg”

Since most of my winner picks are probably wrong I’m going to see if these stats stand tonight

Happy Oscars night:

mattdpearce.substack.com/p/exit-holly...
ok but can we talk about the mystery millionaire of osaka giving the city a bunch of gold to maintain the sewer system

we know our zoomer war videos are terrible but if you hate them you hate America 

www.wsj.com/politics/pol...
FCC Chair Brendan Carr threatens broadcast licenses over Iran War coverage
“But, as you see, it's a beautiful day, the Strait of Hormuz is open and people are having a wonderful time.”

NEW: Our team of FT reporters across the world dug into the strike on the school in Minab. Along the way, we explored what the dearth of real-time, on-the-ground information means for open-source research in this conflict:

as.ft.com/r/f29c784b-3...
The Strait of Hormuz is open for transit
This is the best lede I've seen in a tech story in...years? There should be an award just for ledes.
